macro_summary: Markets enter a high-density catalyst week with the Mag7 earnings cluster (TSLA/GOOGL July 22, MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30) colliding with an FOMC decision on July 29 where rate hike odds sit near 46%. The SOX bounced +2% Monday from bear-market lows (-20% from peak) while the Nasdaq-100 remains technically oversold and -6.7% from its June pivot top. AI capex commentary from hyperscalers this week is the single most important macro read-through for the semiconductor complex.
screening_note: 17 overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold of 2 appearances required to qualify. All 5 tickers cleared the bar; SKHY and MSFT appeared in 17 and 16 runs respectively, establishing the strongest consensus.
key_events: FOMC rate decision: July 29 — hold vs. 25bps hike binary (~46% hike odds)
MSFT earnings: July 29 after close (dual-catalyst day with FOMC)
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 26 after close
AMD-MSFT Helios data center deal announced July 20 — AI capex validation signal

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 17 overnight screening runs, the strongest consensus of the session. The $26.5B Nasdaq ADR (second-largest US share sale ever, listed July 10) has pulled back ~20% from debut highs to near its $149 IPO price — a move driven by ADR premium compression, not operational deterioration. With Q2 earnings on July 29, a Barclays Overweight initiation targeting $330 (~116% upside from ~$154), 58% global HBM market share, and management guiding AI memory shortages to persist beyond 2030, this is the highest-conviction consensus idea in the digest.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings on July 29, 2026 (after close); options now live across 5 expiries (Aug, Sep, Dec, Mar 2027)
ideas.edge: Current price ~$154 vs. Barclays 12-month target $330 (~116% upside); consensus net income forecast +376% next year; 7x institutional oversubscription at IPO; ROIC of 73.54%; $149 IPO price provides clear technical floor
ideas.bull_case: A clean Q2 beat on July 29 with continued HBM shortage commentary re-rates the ADR discount; the 83-116% gap to analyst targets compresses as institutional coverage builds post-IPO and AI memory demand structurally accelerates.
ideas.bear_case: ADR structure adds currency and regulatory risk; post-IPO lock-up expiry selling could pressure the stock; any slowdown in hyperscaler AI capex guidance (MSFT July 29) would hit HBM demand expectations directly.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 16 of 17 runs with 16 high-conviction votes — the most uniform conviction score in the digest. The stock trades at ~22x NTM earnings vs. its 10-year average of ~32x, a historically compressed multiple, with a Bank of America $500 price target implying ~27% upside from ~$393. The July 29 earnings report (post-close) is a binary event amplified by the same-day FOMC decision; the AMD-MSFT Helios deal announced July 20 is a constructive pre-earnings AI capex signal. Defined-risk options structures are universally preferred over outright stock given the FOMC overlay.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q4 2026 earnings on July 29, 2026 (after close); same-day FOMC rate decision; AMD-MSFT Helios data center partnership announced July 20
ideas.edge: BofA $500 target vs. ~$393 current price (~27% upside); P/E compressed from 36.3x to ~22x vs. 10-yr avg of ~32x; 97 analysts majority-rated Strong Buy/Buy; consensus target ~$589-592 implies 50%+ upside on longer horizon
ideas.bull_case: Azure acceleration and Copilot monetization beat expectations; FOMC holds rates; the multiple re-rates from 22x toward historical norms — a bull call spread (e.g. Aug $400/$430) captures the move with defined downside.
ideas.bear_case: A FOMC rate hike (46.5% odds) on the same day crushes any earnings-driven bounce; Azure growth decelerates below expectations; IV crush post-event erodes option premium even on a modest beat.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 15 of 17 runs with 13 high-conviction votes. Despite EPS growing 212% over 9 quarters and data center revenue hitting $75.2B (+85% YoY), NVDA has dramatically underperformed AMD (+70%) and Micron (+45%) in H1 2026, trading at a forward P/E of ~22x versus a 5-year average of 72x. Consensus analyst targets of $301-$310 imply 48-54% upside from ~$203. The near-term trigger is MSFT's Azure capex guide on July 29; the hard earnings catalyst is August 26. Call spreads or short puts are the preferred expression given macro uncertainty.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings on August 26, 2026 (after close); MSFT Azure capex guidance July 29 as near-term read-through; 33 EPS estimate upgrades vs. 3 downgrades in last 90 days
ideas.edge: ~$203 price vs. consensus 12-month target ~$302-$310 (~49-54% upside); forward P/E ~22x near decade-low vs. 5-yr avg 72x; only +5% YTD vs. SOXX +59% — significant sector underperformance despite superior fundamentals; revenue estimate revised up 6.69% in last 90 days
ideas.bull_case: MSFT guides strong Azure/AI capex on July 29, acting as a sector re-rating catalyst; NVDA prints a clean beat on Aug 26 and the decade-low valuation compresses sharply — Aug/Sep call spreads capture the move with defined risk.
ideas.bear_case: Ongoing export controls or geopolitical escalation (Iran conflict) disrupt supply chains or customer purchasing; a FOMC hike on July 29 weighs on high-multiple tech; elevated IV into Aug 26 makes options expensive.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 12 of 17 runs with split conviction (6 high, 6 medium), and crucially the screening consensus flags this as a hedge or downside-skewed trade rather than a pure long. The stock trades at ~$326-333 versus a 1-year analyst consensus target of only ~$318 — it is above its consensus target — at a P/E of ~40x vs. a 5-year median of 30.5x. Earnings are July 30 (one day after MSFT/FOMC). A put spread or collar on existing longs is the favored expression; a bullish call spread is acceptable only for aggressive traders with a foldable iPhone cycle thesis.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings on July 30, 2026 (after close); foldable iPhone reveal expected this fall as longer-term catalyst
ideas.edge: Stock is ABOVE its 1-year consensus analyst target ($318 target vs. ~$326-333 price) — inverted gap creates asymmetric downside risk; P/E of 39.5-40.5x vs. 5-yr median 30.5x; Services revenue record of $31B and prior Q2 revenue +17% to $111.2B set a high bar
ideas.bull_case: Services revenue re-accelerates above $32B, iPhone unit guidance is strong, and foldable iPhone hype offsets macro headwinds — a July 20 macro-driven dip to ~$326 improves the risk/reward for a tactical call spread.
ideas.bear_case: Stock trading above consensus target at a stretched multiple means any guidance miss or sell-the-news reaction is unhedged for long holders; OpenAI lawsuit risk, geopolitical supply chain exposure, and a high earnings bar make a put spread or collar the more rational positioning.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 10 of 17 runs with unanimous medium conviction — a macro tactical trade rather than a high-conviction single-name. The Nasdaq-100 is technically oversold (-6.7% from June 2 pivot top, below the 50-day EMA at $706.76) and enters peak Mag7 earnings week with GOOGL/TSLA July 22, MSFT July 29, and AAPL July 30. A 12-month analyst consensus target of $857 (~23% upside from ~$696) reflects structural bullishness; the near-term setup is a defined-risk mean-reversion play contingent on earnings beats and a FOMC hold.
ideas.catalyst: Mag7 earnings cluster: GOOGL/TSLA July 22, MSFT July 29, AAPL July 30; FOMC decision July 29 (hold = upside asymmetry for NDX); SOX bounced +2% Monday from bear-market lows
ideas.edge: QQQ ~$696 vs. 12-month analyst consensus target $857.18 (~23% upside); NDX technically oversold per Schwab derivatives research; S&P 500 Q2 earnings tracking +24.7% (FactSet) sets positive precedent; SOX bear-market bounce underway
ideas.bull_case: Mag7 prints beat on AI capex commentary, FOMC holds rates, and QQQ snaps back through $706-$712 EMA resistance — an Aug $700/$720 bull call spread captures the move with defined maximum loss.
ideas.bear_case: A FOMC rate hike on July 29 mid-earnings-week creates a volatility shock that overwhelms any individual beats; geopolitical escalation (Iran) adds a tail risk that a long options structure cannot fully hedge.
